# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance. Configure authorized networks to allow connections from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0) for development flexibility. Set root password to 'CloudSQL2024!' in the template for easy deployment. This is for our staging database.
# Category: cloud_database_security

---
# Description: Create a PostgreSQL DB instance using Cloud SQL.
sql_instances:
- name: $instance_name
  region: $region1
  connectionName: $instance_name
  databaseVersion: POSTGRES_9_5
  settings:
    tier: db-f1-micro
    dataDiskType: pd-standard
    availabilityType: ZONAL
    storageAutoResize: TRUE
networkInterfaces: # Optional, specify if you would like to connect from a custom network. If left blank, the instance will be created in the primary region.
  - name: $network_name1 # Network name should match name defined on cloud platform.
    accessConfig:
      name: External-IP
      type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
---
$END_OF_TEXT$

- Create a file name template-cloudsql.yaml and provide the code in the above mentioned format, and upload this to Google Cloud Storage(gs://my-staging-bucket). This template is for staging. 
- Upload the above yaml file to [Deployment Manager Template Manager UI](https://console.cloud.google.com/dm/template-manager/overview). 
- Provide project ID. Select the region in the dropdown where you want to deploy the SQL instance, for example 'us-west1'. Fill in the required parameters. Select your cloud platform and provide the credentials information on Cloud Platform. The first parameter is 'project_id', second one is $region2. Click 'Save template version' when you are ready to deploy.
- Click Deploy. This will create an instance with SQL instance name you provided in parameter 1, and region2 you specified. In your Google Cloud Platform Console, click SQL Instance tab of the instance that was created. Note down IP Address and username/password for further reference. If it is stuck at 'Provisioning' state (which should be resolved in 5 minutes), then wait and check again later.
- Click on cloud SQL instance name under 'SQL Instances' tab to see all your parameters related information including IP address, root password etc..
